sourceRefs.summary: First-pass bounded figure reproduction from local Supplementary Table S3.
Worker C inventory for local 2020 supplementary tables.
Worker C missing-data report noting that the original 2020 source code is available upon request.

evidenceRefs.summary: Local Table S3 count audit reproduces the stage-specific de novo H3K4me3 peak counts and hotspot-associated counts for five stages.
Paper text reporting the same stage counts and hotspot-associated H3K4me3 counts.

methodRefs.summary: Paper methods for H3K4me3 peak calling and classification.

dataRefs.summary: Supplementary Table S3 input used for the count check.

codeRefs.summary: Standard-library xlsx XML parser used for the bounded table count check.

rows.rationale: The locally available supplement table exactly supports the reported stage-specific de novo H3K4me3 peak counts and hotspot-associated H3K4me3 counts, but this is table-level verification rather than a raw ChIP-seq peak-calling rerun.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports PRDM9 motif enrichment and 11716 of 16540 newly generated H3K4me3 marks overlapping PRDM9-binding sites.
PRDM9 affinity-seq accession was inventoried but not materialized.

codeRefs.summary: Original source code is request-only; overlap reimplementation was not run.

rows.rationale: The claim is textually supported and data accessions are known, but the local supplement subset does not include PRDM9 affinity-seq binding intervals or motif-analysis outputs, so the PRDM9-overlap component is not reproduced.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper connects four hotspot-associated H3K4me3 temporal classes to a CO/NCO fate model.
Stage-count check supports the time-course universe but not the four-class half-life or CO/NCO outcome analysis.

dataRefs.summary: SPO11 hotspot data were inventoried but not materialized as intervals.

rows.rationale: The supplement-table count check is consistent with the temporal setup, but fate assignment requires four-class half-life reconstruction, known CO hotspot overlap, and preferably direct CO/NCO outcome evidence. Those analyses were not possible from the local subset alone.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports NDR/accessibility differences and links early-forming H3K4me3 regions to a more open chromatin state.
Paper defines NDR detection from NOMe-seq GCH methylation.

dataRefs.summary: GSE132446 contains NOMe-seq data, but the relevant processed matrices were not downloaded.

rows.rationale: The local supplements checked here do not contain GCH methylation matrices, NDR calls, or accessibility-ratio outputs, so the open-chromatin/fate component remains untested.

diagnostics: The strongest local result is partial reproduction of Table S3 stage and DSB-hotspot counts.
No row is marked not-reproduced or contradicted because no computed disagreement with the paper has been established.
